mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various agent frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for new users.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI and makes it executable.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using GKE with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Cordon and drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrades the Kagenti release.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Helm chart version may not exist or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm repo is added correctly and the version is valid.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release of Kagenti with support for deployment and orchestration of AI agents.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
